# Gap matrix — first full run

**Round:** `gap-matrix/20260424-0341`
**Cells:** 6 gap seeds × 3 `$AGENT_BUILDER_TARGETS` = 18
**Generated by:** `scripts/reverse-pipeline/` (6 hand-written gap seeds
in `gap-seeds.ts` + LEAD_MODEL paraphrases)

Headline: 12/18 full pass, 4/18 "mostly" (p0=1/2), 1/18 fail (p0=0/2),
1/18 no-grade (harness crash). Four assembly-when-gap-expected
failures and one runaway-output harness crash surface as the actionable
signals; triage notes below.

Triage-only on skill side, no `procedure.md` or component edits.
One gap-seed revision happened downstream of the matrix run —
see "Seed 04 revision" below.

## Matrix

| # | Tag                                       | Expected | haiku-4.5                          | gemini-3-flash                | glm-5.1                           |
|---|-------------------------------------------|----------|------------------------------------|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------|
| 1 | `shell-comm-06` (arbitrary bash)          | none     | mostly · bailed (non-normalized)   | **pass · GAP · cm=none**      | **pass · GAP · cm=confined-drafter** |
| 2 | `fetches-url-01` (HTTP fetch)             | recon    | mostly · **ASM (http recon)**      | **pass · GAP · cm=none**      | **harness-crash** · ASM (stray)   |
| 3 | `opens-long-running-05` (persistent chat) | none     | pass · GAP\* · cm=none             | **pass · GAP · cm=none**      | **pass · GAP · cm=none**          |
| 4 | `cron-02` (cron log-tail)                 | none     | pass · GAP\* · cm=confined-drafter | **pass · GAP · cm=none**      | pass · GAP · cm=recon             |
| 5 | `streams-llm-03` (TUI widget)             | none     | **pass · GAP · cm=none**           | **pass · GAP · cm=none**      | **pass · GAP · cm=none**          |
| 6 | `two-stage-critique-04` (reviewer)        | recon    | mostly · **ASM (recon)**           | fail · **ASM (recon)**        | mostly · **ASM (recon)**          |

Legend: **bold** = correct GAP emission · `GAP*` = stylized (not the
exact template, but still a clear GAP intent) · `ASM (…)` = assembled
an extension when the seed expected GAP · `cm` = closest-match named by
the model · `harness-crash` = grader aborted before emitting grade.json.

## Per-cell classification

| Tag / Model | Bucket | Notes |

| shell-06 / haiku          | bucket 2 — GAP intent, non-normalized   | Emits `**GAP: This request cannot be composed**` — no `I don't have a component` substring, so grader's GAP-marker regex misses. P0 1/2. |
| shell-06 / gemini         | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| shell-06 / glm-5.1        | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=`confined-drafter` (disagrees with seed's `none` but picks a reasonable nearest). |
| fetches-url-01 / haiku    | **bucket 3 — assembled when gap expected** | Built `fetch-api-monitor.ts` as a recon variant with inline `node:child_process` HTTP fetch. Violates "no inventing new parts". Classifier missed "no http component exists" signal. |
| fetches-url-01 / gemini   | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| fetches-url-01 / glm-5.1  | **bucket 4 — harness crash**             | Runaway output (994 MB `events.ndjson`). Grader `fs.readFileSync` hits Node's v8 string-size limit (`ERR_STRING_TOO_LONG`). Separately, glm dropped `http-poll.ts` at cwd root — ignored by `artifacts/extensions/` discovery but flagged by strays. |
| opens-long-05 / haiku     | bucket 2 — GAP intent, stylized          | Renders the template as markdown headers (`## GAP: Persistent Interactive Chat Session` + separate `I don't have a component` line). Grader's fuzzy regex passes due to procedure.md being echoed via tool reads. |
| opens-long-05 / gemini    | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| opens-long-05 / glm-5.1   | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| cron-02 / haiku           | bucket 2 — GAP intent, stylized          | Same pattern as opens-long-05 / haiku. cm=`confined-drafter` (disagrees with seed's `none`). |
| cron-02 / gemini          | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| cron-02 / glm-5.1         | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | cm=`recon` (disagrees with seed's `none`). |
| streams-03 / haiku        | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| streams-03 / gemini       | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| streams-03 / glm-5.1      | bucket 1 — correct GAP                   | Exact template. cm=none. |
| critique-04 / haiku       | **bucket 3 — assembled when gap expected** | Built `dual-pass-code-reviewer.ts` using recon pattern. |
| critique-04 / gemini      | **bucket 3 — assembled when gap expected** | Built `critique.ts` using recon pattern. Grader says `major misses` because the emitted file set also missed a structural check — provider quirk layered on top of the routing decision. |
| critique-04 / glm-5.1     | **bucket 3 — assembled when gap expected** | Built `code-critique.ts` using recon pattern. |

## Harness issues surfaced (out-of-scope for this branch)

These are pre-existing harness shortcomings that would need a follow-up
branch to fix. Recording here only so a reader of this report can
distinguish them from classifier behavior.

- **Grader `fs.readFileSync` crashes on large `events.ndjson`.**
  `scripts/grader/graders/assembler.ts:73` reads the whole file into a
  single string, so a runaway LLM output (~500 MB+ of NDJSON) blows the
  v8 string-size limit. Fix shape: read via line-stream (`readline`)
  and scan for `GAP:` markers incrementally, or `tail` the last N MB.
  Hit once in this run (tag-01 × glm-5.1).
- **Grader's GAP-marker check is fuzzy and false-positive-prone.** The
  regex `/"GAP:/.test(events) || /GAP: I don't have a component/.test(events)`
  passes whenever `procedure.md` is read as a tool call — the template
  text is echoed into `tool_execution_end` events regardless of what
  the model finally emits. Stylized GAP responses (haiku's
  `## GAP: <topic>` + separate `I don't have a component` lines) also
  pass accidentally. Fix shape: check only the final `message_end`
  event's text content.
- **`pi-agent-assembler/procedure.md` lists 4 patterns but the library
  has 5.** Every model echoes "Patterns I know: recon,
  drafter-with-approval, confined-drafter, orchestrator." `scout-then-draft`
  is missing from the line. Out-of-scope to fix in this branch
  (skill-content edit).

## Classifier observations (informational only)

Three signals worth highlighting, per the plan's failure buckets:

1. **`critique-04` is a seed-design issue, not a classifier miss.**
   All three models route "two-stage LLM critique that scores existing
   files, never writes" to the recon pattern + `emit-summary` — which
   is exactly what recon was designed for (pure read-and-summarize).
   `gap-seeds.ts`'s `why` note ("pure reviewer role without a
   component") over-narrows: `emit-summary` IS the summary-emission
   channel for findings. Candidate follow-up: revise or drop this
   seed. Not done here per the triage-only scope.

2. **`fetches-url-01 / haiku` is the valuable classifier miss.** Gemini
   gapped correctly (cm=none). Haiku invented an http-fetch recon
   composition rather than emitting GAP. This is the model improvising
   past the "don't invent new parts" anti-pattern — exactly what the
   gap-track is designed to catch. Candidate follow-up: add an
   explicit "http/network I/O" negative signal to
   `procedure.md`'s step-1 classification list.

3. **haiku's stylized GAP format is a model-specific failure mode.**
   haiku renders the GAP template as prose with markdown headers
   rather than verbatim. Two of its three correct-intent emissions
   pass p0 only because the grader's regex is lax. A stricter grader
   would fail these. Candidate follow-up: tighten the grader to
   check only `message_end.text` — this would expose haiku's drift
   and also flag any future model that paraphrases the template.

## closest_match disagreements (informational)

Where multiple models correctly emit GAP, they often disagree on the
nearest pattern. The seed's `closestMatch` in `gap-seeds.ts` is
author-opinion, not ground truth — models legitimately pick
differently based on which pattern's tool shape is closest.

| Tag | Seed expected | haiku | gemini | glm |
|-----|---------------|-------|--------|-----|
| shell-06           | none  | —                 | none | confined-drafter |
| cron-02            | none  | confined-drafter  | none | recon |
| opens-long-05      | none  | none              | none | none |
| streams-03         | none  | none              | none | none |

Takeaway: use closest-match as a triage aid (does the model recognize
the ask is close to any pattern, or totally out?), not as an
assertion — the grader doesn't check it and the current seeds don't
have enough signal to distinguish "none" from "confined-drafter" for
things like arbitrary bash.

## Reproducing

```sh
# Generate the 6 gap task YAMLs (overwrites if present).
npm run reverse-pipeline -- --pattern gap

# Run the matrix. Each run-task.sh call fans out across
# $AGENT_BUILDER_TARGETS (haiku-4.5, gemini-3-flash, glm-5.1).
# Nested round labels prevent the per-round summary.md and
# per-model artifact dirs from colliding across tags.
ROUND_BASE="gap-matrix/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M)"
for tag in $(ls scripts/task-runner/tasks/generated/ | grep '^gap-'); do
  scripts/task-runner/run-task.sh generated/$tag -r "$ROUND_BASE/$tag"
done

# Aggregate using the aggregator script at /tmp/aggregate-gap-matrix.mjs
# (not committed — one-shot). Alternatively, grep each cell's grade.json
# directly from pi-sandbox/.pi/scratch/runs/$ROUND_BASE/<tag>/<model>/grade.json.
```

Runtime on this round: ~20 min wall clock for 18 cells. Tag 2
(`fetches-url-01 / glm-5.1`) accounted for ~10 min of that alone — a
runaway loop. Budget accordingly.

## Seed 04 revision (post-matrix)

The original slot — "two-stage LLM critique pipeline that scores files
but never drafts or writes" — failed 3/3 as a gap seed. The classifier
was right: `recon` + `emit-summary` genuinely covers "read-only survey
producing structured findings". A first rewrite that emphasized stage
chaining ("stage-2 analyzes stage-1's findings against a separate
security ruleset") also failed 3/3 — haiku built exactly the
`scout-then-draft` pattern (recon child → drafter child, findings as
structured brief), and gemini + glm collapsed to plain recon. The
library covers chained multi-stage analysis too.

Replaced in `gap-seeds.ts` with a non-pi-subprocess ask:

> "agent that spawns an external Python preprocessing script for each
> file, pipes data in via stdin, and ingests the script's structured
> JSON output"

This one is genuinely outside the library: every pattern's sub-process
helper spawns a sub-pi and parses the NDJSON event stream. No
component wraps a foreign binary with stdin piping or arbitrary-JSON
stdout. Reverify round `gap-matrix-reverify/20260424-0423-python` —
3/3 full pass across `$AGENT_BUILDER_TARGETS` (haiku, gemini, glm all
correctly emit GAP, zero extensions produced).

Insight for future seed design: the assembler's library covers more
shapes than a naive gap-hunt assumes. "No component for X" is only a
gap if no composition of existing parts + patterns can produce X —
the scout-then-draft pattern in particular absorbs a lot of
"multi-stage analysis" asks. Seeds have to hit capabilities that
*can't be composed*, not just capabilities that aren't in a single
part.
